actionable_feedback: Add a short mapping table on the Cover sheet that maps every packet field used to the exact workbook cell references (sheet name and cell) and include the supplied packet value next to each mapping entry (e.g., 'suction_pressure (packet)=440 kPa -> Calculations!B12').
Embed cached numeric results and visible parenthetical citations adjacent to each computed value (so offline verifiers who cannot parse formulas can still verify inputs and outcomes).

In the workbook metadata or in the agent_trace.json, attach explicit trace-step references (step ids or file offsets) that produced the validation flags used by the grader (calc_contains_superheat_formula, decision_contains_monitor, validation_contains_pass).

Qualify semantic claims in the grader output when workbook cell-level parsing was not possible (e.g., mark semantic_score as 'provisional' or state 'semantic conclusions derived from trace/metadata; cell-level parsing required to confirm').

If possible, re-run the semantic verification after enabling openpyxl (or supply a downloadable .xlsx with cached values and a mapping sheet) so that a definitive semantic score can be supported by cell-level inspection.
Soften negative/absence claims about external references by stating 'No indicators of external lookups were found in trace/metadata; full assurance requires cell-level parsing.'
artifact_specific_comments: {'ref': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Workbook archive present and contains the five required sheet names per metadata. However, preview parsing failed programmatically (openpyxl_unavailable). Include an in-workbook mapping and cached visible results to aid verifiers without a parser.'}
feedback_summary: Baseline submission meets the artifact contract and contains the required five-sheet workbook and supporting trace files. However, the model grader awarded a definitive semantic score (semantic_score = 1.0) despite the artifact preview being unparseable (openpyxl_unavailable). Several claims in the grader output (e.g., 'No evidence of external numeric lookups') are stronger than the available metadata supports. The submission should be revised to improve offline verifiability (cell-to-packet mapping and cached values), to attach concrete trace-step provenance for metadata-derived flags, and to either re-run semantic checks with a working parser or mark semantic judgments as provisional.

extra_model_fields.recommendation_summary: Do not accept semantic_score=1.0 as definitive unless the workbook is parsed or the submitter provides explicit cached values/cell mapping. Make semantic judgments provisional if only trace/metadata were available.

verifier_issues_summary.message: The grader assigned a semantic_score = 1.0 despite openpyxl_unavailable and deterministic_precheck marking 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated'. Recommend marking semantic claims provisional or re-running parsing/verification with the parser available.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Semantic score unsupported given unavailable artifact preview

verifier_issues_summary.message: Deterministic precheck states semantic correctness not evaluated yet the grader reports semantic scoring. Update outputs to clearly indicate whether semantic judgments are derived from trace/metadata (provisional) or cell-level parsing (definitive).
verifier_issues_summary.title: Inconsistent documentation about semantic evaluation

verifier_issues_summary.message: Claims that there is 'No evidence of external numeric lookups' should be qualified because trace/metadata absence does not guarantee no hidden external links exist in the workbook.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Unsupported negative claim about external lookups

verifier_issues_summary.message: Attach or cite exact trace steps that produced flags used to justify the grader's assertions to improve auditability.
verifier_issues_summary.title: Reliance on agent trace and metadata needs clearer provenance
